description: PyNaCl is a Python binding to the Networking and Cryptography library (NaCl / libsodium). Provides sealed boxes, signing, key exchange, hashing.

documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd toolboxes/pynacl && python3 pynacl.py --target $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd toolboxes/pynacl && python3 pynacl.py --help`; `cd toolboxes/pynacl && python3 pynacl.py --help`. Python bindings to libsodium (NaCl).
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the crypto category. Fits when the engagement needs: Python bindings to libsodium (NaCl).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: Python bindings to libsodium (NaCl).

name: pynacl

trust.notes: Operator-curated Python library. The pip name is resolved from PyPI; the import name is verified against the registry.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
